beats.activity: Wake up, water plants, make coffee, check work messages
beats.reasoning: Ethan starts his day with his morning routine—tending to his plants is a grounding ritual, and with yesterday's low energy (40/100), a calm start at home sets the right tone before the rainy commute.

beats.activity: Drive to birth center in Interbay, navigating around SR-520 closure
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon, so Ethan leaves early to beat the closure and avoid cross-lake gridlock; the steady rain also justifies extra travel buffer on a wet Tuesday morning.

beats.activity: Midwife shift: support clients in labor, charting, team check-ins
beats.reasoning: Typical morning-to-early-afternoon shift at the birth center; the grounding work from yesterday carries into today, and the rain outside reinforces his focus on indoor, purposeful work.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby café, stay dry indoors
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady until 2pm; Ethan opts for a quick, dry meal near work rather than venturing far, staying practical about the weather.

beats.activity: Return to birth center, finish charting, evening prep for overnight shift
beats.reasoning: Midwife shifts can extend into evening and overnight; Ethan transitions into the later part of his work day, handling documentation and preparing for any evening clients.

beats.activity: Drive home through clearing rain, listen to vinyl on speakers
beats.reasoning: Rain clears by mid-afternoon, so by evening the drive back is easier; Ethan uses the commute to decompress, playing music and letting the work day settle.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner, check on fermentation batch from yesterday
beats.reasoning: Home cooking is Ethan's way to ground himself after a work day; checking on the fermentation batch continues yesterday's meditative brewing hobby.

beats.activity: Eat dinner, listen to Steely Dan album, organize vinyl collection
beats.reasoning: Low energy from yesterday (40/100) suggests Ethan prefers quiet, solitary time tonight over socializing; organizing vinyl and playing music aligns with his passion and current mood.

beats.activity: Wind down: read, reflect on the day, prepare for bed
beats.reasoning: Final wind-down at home before sleep; Ethan settles into reading and quiet reflection, staying at home as required before the sleep beat.
7.5 hours of overnight sleep, ending at 06:30 the next morning; spans the calendar boundary as required and gives Ethan recovery time after a full work day.

diary:
Woke up to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle—the kind that makes you want to stay in bed. But the plants needed water and I had clients waiting. The SR-520 closure threw everything into chaos this morning; I ended up taking the long way around, which meant leaving earlier than planned. Felt rushed, honestly.

At the birth center, things were steady. Supported two clients through early labor, did a lot of charting. There's something grounding about that work, even on a day when the weather's got everyone on edge. Grabbed a Starbucks lunch and just sat there, watching the rain, not thinking too hard.

Drive home was quiet—threw on some vinyl, let the music fill the car as the clouds started breaking up. Made kimchi for dinner, checked on my fermentation batch. Spent the evening with Steely Dan and my records, organizing them by year. It's meditative, that kind of work. Ended the day feeling settled, ready to sleep.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure set a minor anxious tone early—I was worried about being late—but once I got to work, that dissolved. Being around laboring clients has this centering effect on me; it reminds me why I chose this job. The lunch break was necessary; I think I needed to step away and just exist quietly for a bit. The drive home felt restorative, especially once the weather cleared. I made a conscious choice to lean into the domestic, comfortable stuff tonight: cooking, organizing records. I think I needed that after an intense morning and a full shift. By evening, my mood had shifted from slightly frazzled to genuinely content.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
